consistency.damage_type: The split damage (Fire and Radiant) is consistent for spells associated with divine or celestial power.
consistency.scaling: The scaling mechanism (increasing one damage type by 1d6 per spell slot level above 5th) is a standard, clear method for increasing spell power.

quality.description: The description is evocative ('column of divine fire roars down from the heavens') and mechanically precise regarding area of effect (10-foot-radius, 40-foot-high cylinder) and save mechanics.
quality.components: The material component ('pinch of sulfur') is thematic for fire-based magic.
quality.mechanics_clarity: The choice for scaling damage (fire or radiant) adds a minor tactical layer for the caster.

mechanics.save_type: Dexterity saving throw (standard for area-of-effect spells dealing direct damage).
mechanics.base_damage: 4d6 Fire + 4d6 Radiant (Total 8d6 average 36 damage before save). This is appropriate for a 5th-level, no-concentration AoE attack.
mechanics.area_of_effect: 10-foot radius cylinder (20-foot diameter), 40 feet high. This is a substantial area.
mechanics.scaling_implication: At 6th level, the damage becomes 5d6 Fire + 4d6 Radiant OR 4d6 Fire + 5d6 Radiant. This is a moderate but noticeable increase in damage output.

analysis.suggested_improvements: None. The spell is well-defined and mechanically sound within the standard ruleset.
